title: Accurate, timely, and portable: Course-agnostic early prediction of student performance from LMS logs

text.content: "Student Performance” AND “Machine Learning” AND “Early Warning System”

rich_text.plain_text: "Student Performance” AND “Machine Learning” AND “Early Warning System”

multi_select.name: Backtested with data from Previous Students

text.content: Accurate, timely, and portable: Course-agnostic early prediction of student performance from LMS logs

title.plain_text: Accurate, timely, and portable: Course-agnostic early prediction of student performance from LMS logs

tables.heading: Summary Table PRED
rows: Being at Risk of Failing in a Course
1606 students from a Information Management School in Western Europe

[Total Clicks, Clicks (%), Online Sessions, Clicks/Session, Clicks/day, Forum Clicks, Discussions Viewed, Forum Posts, Folder Clicks, Resources Viewed, URLs Viewed, Course Clicks, Assessments Started, Assignments Viewed, Assignments Submitted, Submissions (%), Total Time Online, Average duration of session, Largest Period of Inactivity, Days with 0 clicks, Days with no clicks (%), PercCourse_NLogin (N= 1, …, 10)]

All data is obtained from LMS logs. Each row represents a student-course pairing. For each pairing, the authors first decide what date would represent specific course curation thresholds and create a model for each dataset. 

All features were normalized to range [0,1], authors also perform Feature Selection.Hyper-Parameters used were selected from literature.

Class imbalance is addressed via SMOTE, without generating better results.

LSTM uses a sequential representation of a single feature instead of the whole feature set.
ZeroR, Naive Bayes, CART, Logistic Regression, MLP, SVM, kNN, Random Forest, AdaBoost, GBoost, ExtraTrees, LSTM

Authors do a model for each course duration threshold. Selection of best algorithm is done by comparison of The AUC score.

Then, the classifier is evaluated in terms of comparison against a ZeroR and vs a version of the model trained using oversampling via SMOTE.

Finally, these models are also compared against a different model trained on a different feature set - a sequence of numbers of clicks. While the LSTM does not outright beat the traditional classifiers, it does achieve remarkably competitive performances despite being trained on a single feature.

Approach is also tested by training the same models on a second dataset. Results do not beat original author’s approach.

Exclusive use of LMS logs. 

LSTM approach does not consider additional features.

LSTM approach was not shown to generalize well to different datasets.

Dataset was collected during a Covid-Era so warrants generalization to the back to school context.

Under-explored Feature Extraction Strategies.

No Model Hyper-Parameter Tuning asidde from LSTM.

Selection of 25 equal-length timesteps for LSTM warrants justification.

blocks.text: EWS can identify both students at risk and excellent students. Study uses a year-long dataset and tests 2 types of representation in a course-agnostic manner using various stages of course completion:

text.content: EWS can identify both students at risk and excellent students. Study uses a year-long dataset and tests 2 types of representation in a course-agnostic manner using various stages of course completion:

rich_text.plain_text: EWS can identify both students at risk and excellent students. Study uses a year-long dataset and tests 2 types of representation in a course-agnostic manner using various stages of course completion:

blocks.text: Findings show that it is possible to obtain somewhat reliable models at 25% course completion. LSTM got worsde performances but its use may be benefical since the difference is smaller at earlier stages and, given that it only uses a single feature, may be more practical to obtain.

text.content: Findings show that it is possible to obtain somewhat reliable models at 25% course completion. LSTM got worsde performances but its use may be benefical since the difference is smaller at earlier stages and, given that it only uses a single feature, may be more practical to obtain.
